name: clips-library
description: Per-profile short-video library with posters, comments, view/like counters, transcode-status tracking, a unified clip_profiles model with yt-dlp auto-poll, per-profile videos_limit + sticky skip-list, and URL-only admin flows for creating profiles or pulling single videos. Supersedes tiktok-mirror.

envVars.name: CLIPS_ROOT
envVars.description: Host directory containing per-profile subfolders with .mp4/.web.mp4 files

requiresContract.description: ProfileAdminPanel uses useAuthUser() to gate the admin drawer to admin accounts. Hook signature: returns an object containing isAdmin: boolean. Most auth setups already expose this; if not, wrap your auth state.

requiresContractAuth.description: Since 0.4.0 the list route requires a session token and video/poster routes require a media-scoped ?t= token via verifyTokenLoose. ClipsClient builds playback URLs with mediaToken() from @/lib/mediaToken. Requires authentication module >= 0.3.0.

postInstall: 1. Run DB migration to create clip_likes, clip_views, clip_comments, clip_profiles and clip_profile_skipped tables
2. Mount a host volume at CLIPS_ROOT
4. Install yt-dlp on the host (or in the container that handles downloads) — only required for auto-poll profiles
5. Optional: install the transcoder-pipeline module to auto-convert originals to .web.mp4
6. Optional: schedule a daily cron that POSTs to /api/clips/sync-all to poll every auto_poll=1 profile
